content: {"personas":[{"perspective":"Scientific","name":"Dr. Hiroshi Tanaka","expertise":"Molecular endocrinologist at a university medical center studying autophagy, circadian metabolism, and insulin signaling pathways in rodent and human models. Published RCTs on 16:8 time-restricted eating and ketone body dynamics. His viewpoint anchors claims in mechanistic cellular biology rather than correlational epidemiology.","question_focus":"Mitochondrial biogenesis markers, mTOR/AMPK pathway modulation, ketogenesis thresholds, and dose-response curves from randomized crossover trials measuring HOMA-IR, fasting glucose, and lipid panels."},{"perspective":"Regulatory","name":"Maria Escalante, JD","expertise":"Former FDA Center for Food Safety and Applied Nutrition policy attorney specializing in dietary guidance statements, qualified health claims, and EFSA harmonization. Authored rulemaking commentary on DSHEA boundaries for fasting protocols marketed as interventions. Her perspective clarifies what evidentiary bar distinguishes permitted guidance from unlawful disease claims.","question_focus":"Labeling authority over fasting-branded meal plans, vulnerable-population warning requirements (pregnant, diabetic, eating-disordered), and how Codex Alimentarius standards constrain cross-border marketing of IF apps and nutraceuticals."},{"perspective":"Industry","name":"Rajiv Menon, MBA","expertise":"Former Chief Product Officer at a digital-health fasting app (8M+ users) and advisor to meal-replacement brands. Expert in adherence telemetry, behavioral nudges, and real-world drop-off curves across commercial IF protocols. His practitioner vantage reveals the gap between controlled-trial efficacy and at-home effectiveness.","question_focus":"12-week retention rates, protocol compliance heterogeneity, monetization impact on user outcomes, and integration of continuous glucose monitors into consumer fasting products."},{"perspective":"Economic","name":"Dr. Eleanor Whitfield","expertise":"Health economist at a LSE-affiliated research unit conducting cost-effectiveness analyses and Markov modeling of dietary interventions for type 2 diabetes prevention. Specializes in QALY calculations and productivity loss estimates. Her macro-level lens quantifies whether IF delivers measurable payer and societal savings.","question_focus":"Incremental cost-effectiveness ratios versus metformin and GLP-1 agonists, workplace absenteeism effects, insurance reimbursement feasibility, and budget-impact projections over 10-year time horizons."},{"perspective":"Public_Health","name":"Dr. Amara Okonkwo, MPH","expertise":"Population epidemiologist at a state department of health running community-based participatory studies on food insecurity, shift workers, and chrononutrition disparities. Trained in implementation science and qualitative interviewing. Her angle surfaces equity, cultural fit, and unintended harms often invisible in efficacy trials.","question_focus":"Differential outcomes across socioeconomic strata, interaction with Ramadan and religious fasting traditions, eating-disorder relapse signals in adolescents, and scalability within SNAP and WIC programs."}]}
